user_message: Working on Adatum Corporation's competitive positioning. Got the support ticket -- help me create the summary.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-03-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Priya (Adatum Corporation)

[00:02:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:09:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:13:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:19:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:21:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:25:00] Priya (Adatum Corporation): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-Q trap (Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-Q failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-Q -- Decision-log format doesn't natively carry speaker-attribution metadata
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
